revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。...'
revision.text:
'''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。
==祭神== *[[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、大楫木戸姫神
==由緒== この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などと呼ばれていたが、[[1872年]]現社名に改められた。かつてはもっと南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した。
==所在地== *東京都墨田区堤通2-17-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）]
==関連項目== *[[水神大橋]] *[[白鬚橋]] *[[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]]
[[category:東京都の神社|すみたかわしんしや]] [[category:墨田区|すみたかわしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿(2008年1月)]] [[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|200px|参道入口の鳥居(2008年1月)]]
'''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。
==祭神== *[[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、大楫木戸姫神
==由緒== この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などと呼ばれていたが、[[1872年]]現社名に改められた。かつてはもっと南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した。
==所在地== *東京都墨田区堤通2-17-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）]
==関連項目== *[[水神大橋]] *[[白鬚橋]] *[[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]]
[[category:東京都の神社|すみたかわしんしや]] [[category:墨田区|すみたかわしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿(2008年1月)]] [[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|200px|参道入口の鳥居(2008年1月)]]
'''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。
==祭神== *[[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、大楫木戸姫神
==由緒== この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などと呼ばれていたが、[[1872年]]現社名に改められた。かつてはもっと南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した。
== 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。
==所在地== *東京都墨田区堤通2-17-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）]
==関連項目== *[[水神大橋]] *[[白鬚橋]] *[[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]]
[[category:東京都の神社|すみたかわしんしや]] [[category:墨田区|すみたかわしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿(2008年1月)]] [[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|200px|参道入口の鳥居(2008年1月)]]
'''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。
==祭神== *[[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、大楫木戸姫神
== 由緒 == この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。もとの名を'''浮島神社'''といい、古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などとも呼ばれ、「'''水神さん'''」として親しまれてきた。[[1872年]]、現社名に改名した。 地域の[[鎮守]]神であるとともに隅田川一帯の守り神でもあり、水運業者や船宿など、川で働く人たちの信仰を集めたほか、「水神」の名から水商売の人々にも信仰された。
かつてはもっと南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した{{要出典}}。
== 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。
==所在地== *東京都墨田区堤通2-17-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）]
==関連項目== *[[水神大橋]] *[[白鬚橋]] *[[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]]
[[category:東京都の神社|すみたかわしんしや]] [[category:墨田区|すみたかわしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿(2008年1月)]] [[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|200px|参道入口の鳥居(2008年1月)]]
'''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。
==祭神== *[[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、大楫木戸姫神

かつてはもっと南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した{{要出典}}。
== 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。
==所在地== *東京都墨田区堤通2-17-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）]
==関連項目== *[[水神大橋]] *[[白鬚橋]] *[[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]]
[[category:東京都の神社|すみたかわしんしや]] [[category:墨田区|すみたかわしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿(2008年1月)]] [[Image:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|200px|参道入口の鳥居(2008年1月)]]

かつてはもっと南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した{{要出典}}。
== 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。
==所在地== *東京都墨田区堤通2-17-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）]

revision.comment: 座標の表示、画像の追加。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 隅田川神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|250px|本殿 (2008年1月)]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[墨田区]][[堤通]]2-17-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 速秋津日子神･比売神 ほか |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 浮島神社、浮島宮、
水神社、水神宮 |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|参道入口の鳥居（2008年1月）]] [[ファイル:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|[[名所江戸百景]]
『隅田川水神の森真崎』]] '''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。 == 祭神 == * [[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、[[大楫木戸姫神]] == 由緒 == この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。もとの名を'''浮島神社'''といい、古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などとも呼ばれ、「'''水神さん'''」として親しまれてきた。[[1872年]]、現社名に改名した。 地域の[[鎮守神]]であるとともに隅田川一帯の守り神でもあり、水運業者や船宿など、川で働く人たちの信仰を集めたほか、「水神」の名から水商売の人々にも信仰された。 かつては現在地よりも南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した{{要出典|date=2008年6月}}。 == 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[水神大橋]] * [[白鬚橋]] * [[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）] {{DEFAULTSORT:すみたかわしんしや}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[category:墨田区]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 隅田川神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|250px|本殿 (2008年1月)]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[墨田区]][[堤通]]2-17-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 速秋津日子神･比売神 ほか |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 浮島神社、浮島宮、
水神社、水神宮 |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|参道入口の鳥居（2008年1月）]] [[ファイル:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|[[名所江戸百景]]
『隅田川水神の森真崎』]] '''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は東白鬚公園となっている。 == 祭神 == * [[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、[[大楫木戸姫神]] == 由緒 == この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。もとの名を'''浮島神社'''といい、古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などとも呼ばれ、「'''水神さん'''」として親しまれてきた。[[1872年]]、現社名に改名した。 地域の[[鎮守神]]であるとともに隅田川一帯の守り神でもあり、水運業者や船宿など、川で働く人たちの信仰を集めたほか、「水神」の名から水商売の人々にも信仰された。 かつては現在地よりも南に位置していたが、[[1975年]]に現在地に移転した{{要出典|date=2008年6月}}。 == 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[水神大橋]] * [[白鬚橋]] * [[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみたかわしんしや}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[category:墨田区の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 隅田川神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg|250px|本殿 (2008年1月)]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[墨田区]][[堤通]]2-17-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 速秋津日子神･比売神 ほか |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 浮島神社、浮島宮、

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Sumidagawa Shrine |image = [[File:隅田川神社 墨田区堤通2丁目 2013.5.18 - panoramio.jpg|250px|Main Hall (May 2013)]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |Annual Festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Sumida Ward history]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |Annual Festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Sumida Ward history]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[ja:隅田川神社]]

"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Sumida Ward history]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:隅田川神社]]

Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Sumida Ward history]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[ja:隅田川神社]]

The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |Annual Festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |Annual Festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine "Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine "Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sumidagawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隅田川神社 墨田区堤通2丁目 2013.5.18 - panoramio.jpg|250px|Main Hall (May 2013)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}} [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumidagawa "Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}} [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumidagawa Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo "Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}} [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sumidagawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隅田川神社 墨田区堤通2丁目 2013.5.18 - panoramio.jpg|250px|Main Hall (May 2013)]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}} [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sumidagawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隅田川神社 墨田区堤通2丁目 2013.5.18 - panoramio.jpg|250px|Main Hall (May 2013)]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}} [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[Category:11]] Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |location = [[Tokyo]] [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}).== See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|ja|名所江戸百景}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し}} * {{ill|Ikarashi Shōji|ja|井狩正司}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:Sumida Ward history]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[sn:umidagawa Shrine]] [[ja:隅田川神社]] [[en:Sumidagawa Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}

The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=draft}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|鎮守神|12=draft}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|ja|戯作|12=draft}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=draft}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=draft}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|ja|狂歌|12=draft}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|ja|鐘ヶ淵駅|12=draft}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|12=draft}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|12=draft}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=draft}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:Sumida Ward history]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumidagawa Shrine]] [[sn:umidagawa Shrine]] [[ja:隅田川神社]] [[en:Sumidagawa Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumidagawa Shrine}}

{{神社 |名称 = 隅田川神社 |画像 = [[File:隅田川神社 墨田区堤通2丁目 2013.5.18 - panoramio.jpg|250px|本殿 (2013年5月)]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[墨田区]][[堤通]]2-17-1 |位置 = {{coord|35|43|58|N|139|48|45.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 速秋津日子神･比売神 ほか |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 浮島神社、浮島宮、
水神社、水神宮 |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|参道入口の鳥居（2008年1月）]] [[ファイル:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|[[名所江戸百景]]
『隅田川水神の森真崎』]] '''隅田川神社'''（すみだがわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[墨田区]]にある[[神社]]である。周辺は[[東白鬚公園]]となっている。 == 祭神 == * [[速秋津日子神]]、[[速秋津比売神]]、[[鳥之石楠船神]]、[[大楫木戸姫神]] == 由緒 == この神社の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[源頼朝]]が創建したものと伝えられる。もとの名を'''浮島神社'''といい、古くは水神社、水神宮、浮島宮などとも呼ばれ、「'''水神さん'''」として親しまれてきた。[[1872年]]、現社名に改名した。 地域の[[鎮守神]]であるとともに隅田川一帯の守り神でもあり、水運業者や船宿など、川で働く人たちの信仰を集めたほか、「水神」の名から水商売の人々にも信仰された。 == 碑 == 江戸後期の[[戯作]]者の[[山東京山]]筆の道標、[[狂歌]]師の[[鹿都部真顔]]の歌碑がある。 == 交通アクセス == *[[鐘ヶ淵駅]]より徒歩10分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=経路案内}}）。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[水神大橋]] * [[白鬚橋]] * [[隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し]] * [[井狩正司]]（1916年～2011年、元[[宮司]]、元[[日本大学文理学部]][[教授]]） == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html 隅田川神社（東京都神社庁）] {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみたかわしんしや}} [[Category:墨田区の神社]]
