Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Tsutsumi-Dōri|qid=Q11428012}} }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo]]
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]], the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period [[:en:Gesaku|Gesaku]] writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of [[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kanegafuchi Station|Kanegafuchi Station]] ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|qq=|1=Suijin no Watashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658843}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Ikari|qq=}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科|1=Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120920}}) == External Links == * The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]], the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period [[:en:Gesaku|Gesaku]] writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Tsutsumi-Dōri|WD=Q11428012}} }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo]]
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]], the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period [[:en:Gesaku|Gesaku]] writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of [[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kanegafuchi Station|Kanegafuchi Station]] ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|1=Suijin no Watashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658843}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Ikari|qq=}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科|1=Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120920}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|隅田川神社|version=101889294|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] }} "Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]], the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period [[:en:Gesaku|Gesaku]] writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of [[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kanegafuchi Station|Kanegafuchi Station]] ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Tsutsumi-Dōri|WD=Q11428012}} }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo]]
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumida Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local [[:en:Chinjugami|Chinjugami]], the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period [[:en:Gesaku|Gesaku]] writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of [[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kanegafuchi Station|Kanegafuchi Station]] ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|1=Suijin no Watashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658843}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Ikari|qq=}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科|1=Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120920}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine Office)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|隅田川神社|version=101889294|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:隅田川神社]] {{Commons category|Sumidagawa Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11658854}} The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=Chinjugami}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|en|Gesaku|WD=Q1047433|lt=Gesaku}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|en|Kyōka|WD=Q3201017|lt=Kyōka}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|en|Kanegafuchi Station|WD=Q6361921|lt=Kanegafuchi Station}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|1=Suijin no Watashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658843}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Ikari|qq=}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科|1=Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120920}}) == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/13_sumida/13014.html Sumidagawa Shrine (Tokyo Shrine "Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=Chinjugami|1=Chinjugami}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|en|Gesaku|WD=Q1047433|lt=Gesaku|1=Gesaku}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|en|Kyōka|WD=Q3201017|lt=Kyōka|1=Kyōka}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|en|Kanegafuchi Station|WD=Q6361921|lt=Kanegafuchi Station|1=Kanegafuchi Station}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|1=Suijin no Watashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658843}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Ikari|qq=}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科|1=Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120920}}) == External Links == [[co:commons:Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[ja:隅田川神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Sumidagawa Shrine]][[ja:隅田川神社]] [[ja:隅田川神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sumidagawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sumida gawa jinjya1.jpg |location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} [[Sumida Ward]] [[Tsutsumi-dori]] 2-17-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.732778|N|139.812722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} and {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = |Established = |Main Hall = |alternative names = Ukishima Shrine, Ukishima-gū,
Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Tsutsumi-Dōri|WD=Q11428012|1=Tsutsumidōri}} }} [[File:Sumida gawa jinjya2.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii Gate at the Entrance (January 2008)]] [[File:100 views edo 035.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|en|One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|WD=Q165190|lt=One Hundred Famous Views of Edo|1=One Hundred Famous Views of Edo}}
"Matsukaze Shrine at Suijin Grove, Sumida River"]] '''Sumidagawa Shrine''' (隅田川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumida Ward]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The surrounding area is part of {{ill|Higashi-Shirahige Park|ja|東白鬚公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Shirahige Park|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|ja|速秋津日子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|ja|速秋津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|lt=Torinoiwakusufune-no-Kami|ja|鳥之石楠船神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja|大楫木戸姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkajikidohime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The founding of this shrine is unknown, but legend attributes its establishment to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}. Originally named '''Ukishima Shrine''', it was historically referred to by various names such as Suijin Shrine, Suijin-gū, and Ukishima-gū, and has been affectionately called "Suijin-sama." In 1872, it was renamed to its current title. In addition to serving as the local {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=Chinjugami|1=Chinjugami}}, the shrine is considered the guardian deity of the Sumida River area. It has attracted devotion from water transportation workers, boatmen, and people working in the water trade due to its association with "water gods." == Stone Monuments == The shrine grounds feature a signpost written by Edo-period {{ill|Gesaku|en|Gesaku|WD=Q1047433|lt=Gesaku|1=Gesaku}} writer {{ill|Santō Kyōzan|ja|山東京山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santō Kyōzan|qq=}} and a poetry monument dedicated to {{ill|Kadosu Manako|ja|鹿都部真顔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadosu Manako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿津部真顔}}, a master of {{ill|Kyōka|en|Kyōka|WD=Q3201017|lt=Kyōka|1=Kyōka}}. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kanegafuchi Station|en|Kanegafuchi Station|WD=Q6361921|lt=Kanegafuchi Station|1=Kanegafuchi Station}} ({{OSM map route|start=35.73337,139.81991|goal=35.73274,139.81277|name=Route Directions}}). == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige Bridge|ja|白鬚橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Bridge|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|ja|隅田川の渡し#水神の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumida River Ferries#Suijin no Watashi|1=Suijin no Watashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658843}} * {{ill|Masashi Ikari|ja|井狩正司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Ikari|qq=}} (1916–2011, former chief priest and former professor at {{ill|Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja|日本大学文理学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本大学文理学部・大学院文学研究科及び総合基礎科学研究科|1=Nihon University College of Humanities and Sciences|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120920}}) == External Links ==
